stem: Let \( F \) be the antiderivative of \( f(x) = ([[a]])x^{2} + ([[b]])x + ([[c]]) \) satisfying \( F(0) = 0 \). Find \( F([[m]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( f([[m]]) \), the integrand's value, not the antiderivative \( F([[m]]) \).
This omits the \( \tfrac{1}{3} \) and \( \tfrac{1}{2} \) factors from integrating each power.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Let \( F \) be the antiderivative of \( f(x) = \dfrac{[[c]]}{x + [[a]]} \) satisfying \( F(0) = 0 \). Find \( F([[m]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract the value of the antiderivative at the lower limit \( x=0 \).
This divides by \( [[a]] \) instead of using it inside the logarithm's argument.
This drops the leading coefficient \( [[c]] \) entirely.
This multiplies by \( [[a]] \) instead of leaving it inside the log's argument.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This divides out the coefficient \( [[c]] \) instead of keeping it as a factor.

stem: Let \( F \) be the antiderivative of \( f(x) = [[c]]e^{[[a]]x} \) satisfying \( F(0) = 0 \). Find \( F([[m]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( f([[m]]) \), the integrand at \( [[m]] \), instead of the antiderivative.
This multiplies by \( [[a]] \) instead of dividing by it.
This forgets to subtract the value of the antiderivative at \( x=0 \).
This drops the \( \tfrac{1}{[[a]]} \) factor entirely.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This divides the correct value by \( [[a]] \) an extra time.

stem: A student claims that \( F(x) = [[a]]x^{4} + [[b]]x^{3} + [[c]]x^{2} \) is an antiderivative of some function \( f \). Verify the claim by computing \( F'([[x0]]) \), which must equal \( f([[x0]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( F([[x0]]) \) itself rather than differentiating first.
This drops the \( 3 \) coefficient on the \( x^{3} \) term's derivative.
This forgets to double the coefficient \( [[c]] \) on the \( x^{2} \) term's derivative.
This differentiates \( F \) a second time (computes \( F''([[x0]]) \)) instead of \( F'([[x0]]) \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The function \( F(x) = [[a]]x^{3} + ([[b]])x^{2} + C \) is an antiderivative of \( f(x) = 3([[a]])x^{2} + 2([[b]])x \). If \( F([[x1]]) = [[k]] \), find \( C \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract the \( [[b]]x_1^{2} \) term when isolating \( C \).
This adds the polynomial terms instead of subtracting them.
This divides \( k \) by the polynomial value instead of subtracting it.
This has the sign of \( C \) reversed.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
